{"detail":"The bead has precise file:line citations, a clear non-scope, and well-formed ACs, so it is not decomposable. However, the proposed fix contains an unresolved product-semantic fork: 'auto-close only when AC/deps satisfied by closed children' vs. 'surface as closure candidate requiring operator action.' AC2 ('surface or close') and AC3 ('distinguishes completed-epic closure candidates from structural epics with open children') mirror this fork rather than resolving it. Three consecutive review_block attempts produced no implementation changes — a pattern consistent with agents stalling at the fork point rather than committing to either path. Resolving the fork requires a human product decision: whether ddx work should auto-close completed epics or only report them. That choice cannot be made without operator input, so rewriting would require choosing product semantics, which is excluded by intake rules.","intake_outcome":"ambiguous_needs_human"}
{"detail":"pre-claim intake requires a smart/frontier route but no viable model was available","intake_outcome":"ambiguous_needs_human"}
